chunk_summary: This section explores qualitative methods for nutrition implementation research in India, emphasizing ethical consent planning, data privacy, and rigorous analysis frameworks tailored to local contexts.
learning_objectives: Understand qualitative methods for implementation research in nutrition practice.
Learn India-specific ethical guidelines for child research and consent planning.
Explore frameworks for analyzing qualitative data, such as CFIR and COREQ.
Examine strategies for tracking intervention adaptations and fidelity using FRAME.
key_facts: Qualitative methods include semi-structured interviews, focus groups, and workflow observations.
Sampling should ensure variation across urban/rural, language, gender, and caste/tribal status.
Ethics planning must follow ICMR guidelines, including parent permission and child assent.
Data handling must align with India’s DPDP Act to protect privacy and minimize coercion.
Interventions must be registered in CTRI if prospectively assigned.
reference_quote: Follow ICMR national ethical guidelines for child research. Obtain parent permission and age-appropriate child assent; document how assent will be obtained in local language and how dissent will be handled.
document_key_topics: Data privacy and protection in research
Frameworks for intervention fidelity and adaptation
